verses.text: He teaches from a boat as the crowd stands on the shore. Pressing throngs. So many come that He preaches from the water.
He teaches from a boat as the crowd stands on the shore. Pressing throngs. So many come that He preaches from the water.
A sower scatters seed; some falls by the way, some on stony ground. Different soils. The same word meets very different hearts.
A sower scatters seed; some falls by the way, some on stony ground. Different soils. The same word meets very different hearts.
A sower scatters seed; some falls by the way, some on stony ground. Different soils. The same word meets very different hearts.
Some is choked by thorns, some bears fruit; 'he that hath ears to hear, let him hear.' Fruitful ground. Only the good heart yields a harvest.
Some is choked by thorns, some bears fruit; 'he that hath ears to hear, let him hear.' Fruitful ground. Only the good heart yields a harvest.
Some is choked by thorns, some bears fruit; 'he that hath ears to hear, let him hear.' Fruitful ground. Only the good heart yields a harvest.
Some is choked by thorns, some bears fruit; 'he that hath ears to hear, let him hear.' Fruitful ground. Only the good heart yields a harvest.
To the twelve the mystery is given, but to others in parables. Hidden and revealed. The word both opens and closes, by the hearer's heart.
To the twelve the mystery is given, but to others in parables. Hidden and revealed. The word both opens and closes, by the hearer's heart.
To the twelve the mystery is given, but to others in parables. Hidden and revealed. The word both opens and closes, by the hearer's heart.
The seed is the word; the wayside loses it to Satan, the stony springs up quickly. No root. Shallow soil cannot hold the word.
The seed is the word; the wayside loses it to Satan, the stony springs up quickly. No root. Shallow soil cannot hold the word.
The seed is the word; the wayside loses it to Satan, the stony springs up quickly. No root. Shallow soil cannot hold the word.
The seed is the word; the wayside loses it to Satan, the stony springs up quickly. No root. Shallow soil cannot hold the word.
The thorny is choked by care and riches; the good ground bears thirtyfold, sixty, a hundred. Lasting fruit. The honest heart receives and keeps the word.
The thorny is choked by care and riches; the good ground bears thirtyfold, sixty, a hundred. Lasting fruit. The honest heart receives and keeps the word.
The thorny is choked by care and riches; the good ground bears thirtyfold, sixty, a hundred. Lasting fruit. The honest heart receives and keeps the word.
The thorny is choked by care and riches; the good ground bears thirtyfold, sixty, a hundred. Lasting fruit. The honest heart receives and keeps the word.
Is a candle hid under a bushel? nothing is hid that shall not be revealed. Light to shine. Truth is given to be shown, not concealed.
Is a candle hid under a bushel? nothing is hid that shall not be revealed. Light to shine. Truth is given to be shown, not concealed.
Is a candle hid under a bushel? nothing is hid that shall not be revealed. Light to shine. Truth is given to be shown, not concealed.
With what measure ye mete, it shall be measured; to him that hath shall be given. Growing or losing. Use of the word brings increase; neglect, loss.
With what measure ye mete, it shall be measured; to him that hath shall be given. Growing or losing. Use of the word brings increase; neglect, loss.
The kingdom is like seed that grows the man 'knoweth not how,' till the harvest. Hidden growth. God's word works quietly until the day of reaping.
The kingdom is like seed that grows the man 'knoweth not how,' till the harvest. Hidden growth. God's word works quietly until the day of reaping.
The kingdom is like seed that grows the man 'knoweth not how,' till the harvest. Hidden growth. God's word works quietly until the day of reaping.
The kingdom is like seed that grows the man 'knoweth not how,' till the harvest. Hidden growth. God's word works quietly until the day of reaping.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least, yet becoming the greatest. Small beginnings. From a tiny start God's kingdom spreads to shelter all.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least, yet becoming the greatest. Small beginnings. From a tiny start God's kingdom spreads to shelter all.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least, yet becoming the greatest. Small beginnings. From a tiny start God's kingdom spreads to shelter all.

A great storm rises; Jesus sleeps; 'carest thou not that we perish?' Sleeping Lord. He rests while His own are tossed, to try their faith.
A great storm rises; Jesus sleeps; 'carest thou not that we perish?' Sleeping Lord. He rests while His own are tossed, to try their faith.
A great storm rises; Jesus sleeps; 'carest thou not that we perish?' Sleeping Lord. He rests while His own are tossed, to try their faith.
A great storm rises; Jesus sleeps; 'carest thou not that we perish?' Sleeping Lord. He rests while His own are tossed, to try their faith.
He rebukes the wind, 'Peace, be still'; a great calm comes; 'what manner of man is this?' Lord of the storm. Wind and sea obey His word.
